region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche danger will increase quickly in the early morning. A lot of new snow above approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Already in the morning, Fast increase in avalanche danger level, especially in the areas bordering Piedmont. In the course of the day the natural activity of medium and large dry and wet avalanches will rapidly increase. On steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is quite high. The conditions are dangerous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

60 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 2200 m. This situation will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach quite a large size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
In addition large wind slabs will form in particular above the tree line.
At high altitude many medium-sized and, in many cases, large slab avalanches are to be expected as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. This applies in particular in case of releases originating from very steep starting zones at high altitude. Here natural avalanches, capable of reaching a long way, must be expected.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
Several medium-sized to large ground avalanches have been released since Tuesday. This applies in particular on very steep northeast, east facing slopes.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released naturally. Numerous medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In places where a lot of snow falls danger level 4 (high) will be reached in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The snowpack will become unstable in particular on steep slopes.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from southeasterly directions, precarious wind slabs will form in the course of the day in particular in gullies and bowls as well as above the tree line. On steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m. At lower altitudes and on very steep slopes small to medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m. They are bonding poorly with the old snowpack at high altitude.

tendency.highlights: The natural avalanche activity will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular below approximately 2200 m, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes. In these regions natural avalanches are to be expected, even large ones in isolated cases.

In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in some cases, in particular on near-ridge slopes.
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2200 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. At higher altitudes there are 30 to 80 cm of snow, and even as much as 150 cm in some localities. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to 2200 m rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2200 m. As a consequence of a strong wind, sometimes easily released wind slabs will form in high Alpine regions.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m: Large-grained weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Slight decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the snowfall level drops. Above approximately 2000 m snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. Large and, in isolated cases, very large wet slab avalanches are possible in some places.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes above approximately 1800 m. In the regions exposed to a lot of new snow caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and. In addition the fresh wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are capable of being triggered in some locations. At intermediate altitudes the danger is a little lower. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early. Above approximately 1800 m the danger will increase for a short time to level 4 (high) as the day progresses.

snowpackStructure.comment: A lot of snow has fallen over a wide area. In addition sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. Various wind slab layers are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid and thorough wetting of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes also at high altitude. As a consequence of the rain the prevalence and size of these avalanche prone locations will increase in the afternoon.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2200 m. In these regions natural avalanches are to be expected, even large ones.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in isolated cases, in particular on near-ridge shady slopes. Wind slabs are mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. At higher altitudes there are 30 to 80 cm of snow, and even as much as 150 cm in some localities. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. The sleet will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack in some places below approximately 2200 m.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Above approximately 2200 m snow will fall. As a consequence of a strong wind, small wind slabs will form in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: Slight decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the snowfall level drops. Above approximately 2000 m snow will fall in some regions.

bulletin.highlights: As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. Wind slabs in high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular below approximately 2500 m, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes. In these regions natural avalanches are to be expected, even large ones. Moist and wet avalanches can additionally in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant, this also applies on steep west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2800 m.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in some cases, in particular on near-ridge shady slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to 2500 m and above rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2500 m. As a consequence of a strong wind, sometimes easily released wind slabs will form in high Alpine regions.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Slight decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the snowfall level drops. Above approximately 2000 m snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m. In these regions natural avalanches are to be expected, even large ones. Moist and wet avalanches can additionally in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant, this also applies on steep west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2800 m.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m.
In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in isolated cases, in particular on near-ridge shady slopes. Wind slabs are mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to 2500 m rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

Above approximately 2500 m snow will fall. As a consequence of a strong wind, small wind slabs will form in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: Slight decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the snowfall level drops. Above approximately 2000 m snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate wind from northeasterly directions, mostly small wind slabs will form in the course of the day. The fresh wind slabs will be covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise.

The fresh snow and the wind slabs formed during the snowfall can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m. In particular on very steep grassy slopes and in extremely steep terrain small to medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs that are forming at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The fresh snow will be rather moist. Especially in areas with smaller amounts of fresh snow, this will favour bonding with the old snowpack.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

tendency.highlights: Medium-sized natural avalanches are possible on very steep slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche danger will already increase in the early morning. Some new snow above approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
On very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is quite high. The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

30 to 60 cm of snow will fall until the evening above approximately 2200 m. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack. At low altitude and on very steep slopes medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach quite a large size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
In addition sometimes deep wind slabs will form in particular above the tree line.
At high altitude and adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas small and medium-sized slab avalanches are to be expected as a consequence of new snow and wind.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last few days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
Several medium-sized to large ground avalanches have been released since Tuesday. This applies in particular on very steep northeast, east facing slopes.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming at intermediate and high altitudes are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. Moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow represent the main danger. New snow and wind slabs above approximately 2200 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area up to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Monday above approximately 2500 m. Up to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Tuesday. Up to 2000 m rain will fall.
The avalanche prone locations are widespread and are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. The avalanche prone locations for wet avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2200 m. In regions exposed to heavier precipitation these avalanche prone locations are more widespread. From starting zones that have retained some snow in particular the wet avalanches can reach fairly large size.
The new snow can be released naturally in all aspects above approximately 2500 m. In particular on very steep slopes and at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible. Bases of rock walls are especially dangerous.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southeasterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs will form especially above approximately 2500 m. The fresh wind slabs can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m.
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released by winter sport participants on shady slopes. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The sleet will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2200 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: He avalanche danger will increase in the afternoon.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow, and up to 40 cm in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The new snow will become progressively more unstable at intermediate and high altitudes, especially on steep slopes.
Avalanches can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall today above approximately 1900 m.
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs that are forming at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The fresh snow will be rather moist. Especially in areas with smaller amounts of fresh snow, this will favour bonding with the old snowpack.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist avalanches below approximately 1900 m. New snow at elevated altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1900 m.
The rain will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.
Isolated mostly small dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of the new snow. Avalanches can in some places be released, in particular by large loads.
In regions exposed to heavier precipitation and at elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

The fresh snow will be rather moist. This snow will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes. Especially in areas with smaller amounts of fresh snow, this will favour bonding with the old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs of Monday are bonding quite well with the old snowpack in all aspects.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of snowfall above approximately 1800 m and the moderate to strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow is bonding quite well with the old snowpack in all aspects above approximately 2000 m. Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of solar radiation, the natural avalanche activity will gradually increase. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Gliding avalanches can also occur. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. The wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes and generally at elevated altitudes. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. In many places there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snow cover and its thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude: depending on nighttime and daytime radiation, the snow cover may refreeze in many places or become more moist during the day. At low and intermediate altitudes a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes. Individual natural avalanches are to be expected, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to 2200 m and above rain will fall. The weather conditions towards the evening will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Slight decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the snowfall level drops. Above approximately 2000 m snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes. Individual natural avalanches are to be expected, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to 2200 m and above rain will fall. The weather conditions towards the evening will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This situation will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Slight decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the snowfall level drops. Above approximately 2000 m snow will fall in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Up to high altitudes rain will fall over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment: The surface of the snowpack is not frozen and will already be soft in the early morning.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of heavy rain up to high altitudes and the occasionally strong wind, an unfavourable avalanche situation will develop.
